title: Sour Ethiopian Stew
description: A flavorful and spicy stew originating from Ethiopia, made with tender meat and fresh peas.

ingredients.name: Tender beef (cut into bite-sized pieces)
ingredients.notes: You can also use lamb or a combination of the two

ingredients.name: Sour cream
ingredients.notes: used to add a tangy flavor to the stew

ingredients.name: Ethiopian berbere spice blend
ingredients.notes: available at most African or Middle Eastern grocery stores

ingredients.name: Onion, chopped
ingredients.notes: add more or less to taste

ingredients.name: Garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: also add more or less to taste

ingredients.name: Frozen peas
ingredients.notes: fresh peas can be used if available

ingredients.name: Red wine
ingredients.notes: optional, but adds a nice flavor

ingredients.name: Water
ingredients.notes: add more or less as needed

instructions: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of oil over medium-high heat.
Add the chopped onion and sauté until it starts to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and sauté for another minute, until fragrant.
Add the beef and sauté until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the berbere spice blend, tomato paste, and red wine (if using). Stir well to combine.
Add the water and bring the stew to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 hour, or until the beef is tender.
Add the frozen peas and cook for another 10-15 minutes, or until they are tender.
Stir in the sour cream and cook for a few minutes, until heated through.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh cilantro (if desired).
